title: 沉睡谋杀案
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\140-沉睡谋杀案-阿加莎克里斯蒂.txt
summary:
While working in the garden, Foster discovers original steps leading toward the lawn that had been previously filled in. He discusses landscaping with Gwendalyn, noting that while certain shrubs will provide a deep view from the living room window, they will block light and include expensive, old jasmine that cannot be transplanted. When Gwendalyn inquires about the house's previous residents, Foster recounts that before the Hendricks family, the house was occupied by Miss Elvosi, a Low Church believer, and a black pastor. He also mentions Mrs. Findlay, an aristocrat who lived there for many years, planted the magnolias and privet hedges, and eventually died abroad before being buried in the churchyard. Foster expresses dissatisfaction with the constant changes in the area, noting how a local hospital became a girls' school.

Gwendalyn returns inside to write a letter to her cousin, Joan West, declining an invitation to stay in Chelsea because she must oversee workmen at Deermeadow. She grows frustrated by the lack of a door between the living room and the dining room, which forces her to walk through a drafty hall. She consults Mr. Sims, an architect and interior designer, about installing a door. Despite her suspicion regarding his tendency to exceed budgets, Mr. Sims agrees to the improvement. His foreman, Taylor, inspects the wall and reveals that a door originally existed in that exact location but had been blocked up. This discovery causes Gwendalyn unease, as she realizes she has repeatedly and instinctively walked toward the exact spot where the hidden door once stood, leading her to wonder if she possesses psychic abilities or if the house is haunted.
